page: Amazon Web Services x8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by two Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.9 GHz and a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It includes a 75 Gbps baseline network interface but contains no local storage, requiring external storage solutions.

In benchmark evaluations, the x8i.metal-48xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities. The multi-core stress-ng score of 209138.43 and Geekbench multi-core score of 22085.0 place it in the top 10% of tested servers. Read-only memory bandwidth reaches 15496.43 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 525.98 GB/sec for large blocks. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the middle 50% at 2143.3.

This server is designed for memory-intensive, highly parallelized workloads. It achieves top-tier results in database operations, processing 271.51 million Redis SET operations per second, and excels in multi-threaded decompression and software compilation. The inclusion of Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) supports efficient LLM prompt processing, reaching 43.82 tokens/second on 70B models. Given the high density of DDR5 memory and dedicated compute resources, it offers high qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise ERP systems, and parallel data processing pipelines, though the lack of local storage requires accounting for network storage overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server configured with 192 vCPUs across 96 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores and 3072.0 GB of 7 GHz DDR5 memory. It features a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage. The server delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 22085.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 1197.68 GB/sec. Memory read bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 525.98 GB/sec for large blocks, though single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores remain in the average tier. Equipped with Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), it processes LLM prompts efficiently, making it suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy multi-threaded compilation, and enterprise-grade parallel data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 3 TB of DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for in-memory databases and parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs, 3072 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps bandwidth for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-intensive server featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and three terabytes of high-speed DDR5 RAM for massive parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated vCPUs via Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors with 480 MB L3 cache.
3072.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory (16.0 GB per core).
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth up to 525.98 GB/sec.
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage.
Intel AMX support for accelerated mathematical and AI inference workloads.
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis, and parallel data processing.
